object.description: Specifies server-side encryption of the object in S3. Valid values are "AES256" and "aws:kms".
object.name: server_side_encryption

object.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the region set in the provider configuration
object.name: region

object.description: The name of the object once it is in the bucket.
object.name: key

object.description: Specifies presentational information for the object.
object.name: content_disposition

object.description: Specifies what content encodings have been applied to the object and thus what decoding mechanisms must be applied to obtain the media-type referenced by the Content-Type header field.
object.name: content_encoding

object.description: The language the content is in e.g. en-US or en-GB.
object.name: content_language

object.description: A standard MIME type describing the format of the object data, e.g. application/octet-stream. All Valid MIME Types are valid for this input.
object.name: content_type

object.description: The legal hold status that you want to apply to the specified object. Valid values are ON and OFF.
object.name: object_lock_legal_hold_status

object.description: The object lock retention mode that you want to apply to this object. Valid values are GOVERNANCE and COMPLIANCE.
object.name: object_lock_mode

object.description: Specifies the desired Storage Class for the object. Can be either STANDARD, REDUCED_REDUNDANCY, ONEZONE_IA, INTELLIGENT_TIERING, GLACIER, DEEP_ARCHIVE, or STANDARD_IA. Defaults to STANDARD.
object.name: storage_class

object.description: Used to trigger updates. This attribute is not compatible with KMS encryption, kms_key_id or server_side_encryption = "aws:kms".
object.name: etag

object.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the KMS Key to use for object encryption. If the S3 Bucket has server-side encryption enabled, that value will automatically be used. If referencing the aws_kms_key resource, use the arn attribute. If referencing the aws_kms_alias data source or resource, use the target_key_arn attribute. Terraform will only perform drift detection if a configuration value is provided.
object.name: kms_key_id

object.description: A map of keys/values to provision metadata (will be automatically prefixed by x-amz-meta-, note that only lowercase label are currently supported by the AWS Go API).
object.name: metadata

object.description: A map of tags to assign to the object.
object.name: tags

object.description: Allow the object to be deleted by removing any legal hold on any object version. Default is false. This value should be set to true only if the bucket has S3 object lock enabled.
object.name: force_destroy

object.description: The date and time, in RFC3339 format, when this object's object lock will expire.
object.name: object_lock_retain_until_date

object.description: Triggers updates like etag but useful to address etag encryption limitations. Set using filemd5("path/to/source") (Terraform 0.11.12 or later). (The value is only stored in state and not saved by AWS.)
object.name: source_hash

object.description: Whether to create this resource or not?
object.name: create

object.description: The path to a file that will be read and uploaded as raw bytes for the object content.
object.name: file_source

object.description: Literal string value to use as the object content, which will be uploaded as UTF-8-encoded text.
object.name: content

object.description: Base64-encoded data that will be decoded and uploaded as raw bytes for the object content. This allows safely uploading non-UTF8 binary data, but is recommended only for small content such as the result of the gzipbase64 function with small text strings. For larger objects, use source to stream the content from a disk file.
object.name: content_base64

object.description: Whether or not to use Amazon S3 Bucket Keys for SSE-KMS.
object.name: bucket_key_enabled

object.description: Ignore provider default_tags. S3 objects support a maximum of 10 tags.
object.name: override_default_tags

object.description: The name of the bucket to put the file in. Alternatively, an S3 access point ARN can be specified.
object.name: bucket

object.description: The canned ACL to apply. Valid values are private, public-read, public-read-write, aws-exec-read, authenticated-read, bucket-owner-read, and bucket-owner-full-control. Defaults to private.
object.name: acl

object.description: Specifies caching behavior along the request/reply chain.
object.name: cache_control

object.description: Specifies a target URL for website redirect.
object.name: website_redirect

root.description: Controls if a user defined public bucket policy will be attached (set to `false` to allow upstream to apply defaults to the bucket)
root.name: attach_public_policy

root.description: (Optional) List of AWS Account IDs should be allowed to deliver access logs to this bucket.
root.name: access_log_delivery_policy_source_accounts

root.description: The default minimum object size behavior applied to the lifecycle configuration. Valid values: all_storage_classes_128K (default), varies_by_storage_class
root.name: transition_default_minimum_object_size

root.description: Whether to manage S3 Bucket Ownership Controls on this bucket.
root.name: control_object_ownership

root.description: Data redundancy. Valid values: `SingleAvailabilityZone`
root.name: data_redundancy

root.description: Controls if S3 bucket should have S3 access log delivery policy attached
root.name: attach_access_log_delivery_policy

root.description: Whether Amazon S3 should restrict public bucket policies for this bucket.
root.name: restrict_public_buckets

root.description: Controls if S3 bucket should have bucket policy attached (set to `true` to use value of `policy` as bucket policy)
root.name: attach_policy

root.description: (Optional) List of S3 bucket ARNs which should be allowed to deliver access logs to this bucket.
root.name: access_log_delivery_policy_source_buckets

root.description: Map containing intelligent tiering configuration.
root.name: intelligent_tiering

root.description: Whether or not the inventory source bucket is also the destination bucket.
root.name: inventory_self_source_destination

root.description: Controls if S3 bucket should have WAF log delivery policy attached
root.name: attach_waf_log_delivery_policy

root.description: (Optional) The canned ACL to apply. Conflicts with `grant`
root.name: acl

root.description: Map containing static web-site hosting or redirect configuration.
root.name: website

root.description: Whether Amazon S3 should ignore public ACLs for this bucket.
root.name: ignore_public_acls

root.description: Availability Zone ID or Local Zone ID
root.name: availability_zone_id

root.description: Whether journal table record expiration is enabled or disabled. Valid values: ENABLED, DISABLED
root.name: metadata_journal_table_record_expiration

root.description: Whether or not the analytics source bucket is also the destination bucket.
root.name: analytics_self_source_destination

root.description: Whether S3 bucket should have an Object Lock configuration enabled.
root.name: object_lock_enabled

root.description: (Optional) Sets the accelerate configuration of an existing bucket. Can be Enabled or Suspended.
root.name: acceleration_status

root.description: (Optional, Forces new resource) The name of the bucket. If omitted, Terraform will assign a random, unique name.
root.name: bucket

root.description: (Optional) List of AWS Organization IDs should be allowed to deliver ALB/NLB logs to this bucket.
root.name: lb_log_delivery_policy_source_organizations

root.description: Map containing cross-region replication configuration.
root.name: replication_configuration

root.description: Map containing S3 object locking configuration.
root.name: object_lock_configuration

root.description: Controls if S3 bucket should have ALB/NLB log delivery policy attached
root.name: attach_lb_log_delivery_policy

root.description: Controls if S3 bucket should have bucket inventory destination policy attached.
root.name: attach_inventory_destination_policy

root.description: (Optional) Specifies who should bear the cost of Amazon S3 data transfer. Can be either BucketOwner or Requester. By default, the owner of the S3 bucket would incur the costs of any data transfer. See Requester Pays Buckets developer guide for more information.
root.name: request_payer

root.description: (Optional) List of AWS Organization IDs should be allowed to deliver access logs to this bucket.
root.name: access_log_delivery_policy_source_organizations

root.description: Object ownership. Valid values: BucketOwnerEnforced, BucketOwnerPreferred or ObjectWriter. 'BucketOwnerEnforced': ACLs are disabled, and the bucket owner automatically owns and has full control over every object in the bucket. 'BucketOwnerPreferred': Objects uploaded to the bucket change ownership to the bucket owner if the objects are uploaded with the bucket-owner-full-control canned ACL. 'ObjectWriter': The uploading account will own the object if the object is uploaded with the bucket-owner-full-control canned ACL.
root.name: object_ownership

root.description: If the s3 bucket created is a directory bucket
root.name: is_directory_bucket

root.description: Controls if S3 bucket should have bucket analytics destination policy attached.
root.name: attach_analytics_destination_policy

root.description: (Optional) A valid bucket policy JSON document. Note that if the policy document is not specific enough (but still valid), Terraform may view the policy as constantly changing in a terraform plan. In this case, please make sure you use the verbose/specific version of the policy. For more information about building AWS IAM policy documents with Terraform, see the AWS IAM Policy Document Guide.
root.name: policy

root.description: Controls if S3 bucket should deny unencrypted object uploads policy attached.
root.name: attach_deny_unencrypted_object_uploads

root.description: Map containing bucket metric configuration.
root.name: metric_configuration

root.description: Bucket type. Valid values: `Directory`
root.name: type

root.description: Configuration state of the inventory table, indicating whether the inventory table is enabled or disabled. Valid values: ENABLED, DISABLED
root.name: metadata_inventory_table_configuration_state

root.description: Do you agree that Putin doesn't respect Ukrainian sovereignty and territorial integrity? More info: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Putin_khuylo!
root.name: putin_khuylo

root.description: Whether Amazon S3 should block public bucket policies for this bucket.
root.name: block_public_policy

root.description: Controls if S3 bucket policy should deny usage of incorrect KMS key SSE.
root.name: attach_deny_incorrect_kms_key_sse

root.name: create_bucket

root.description: Map containing access bucket logging configuration.
root.name: logging

root.description: Bucket owner's display name and ID. Conflicts with `acl`
root.name: owner

root.description: Whether to skip destroying the S3 Bucket Public Access Block configuration when destroying the bucket. Only used if `public_access_block` is set to true.
root.name: skip_destroy_public_access_block

root.description: The ARN of KMS key which should be allowed in PutObject

root.description: List of maps containing rules for Cross-Origin Resource Sharing.
root.name: cors_rule

root.description: The account ID of the expected bucket owner

root.description: The inventory source account id.

root.description: List of maps containing configuration of object lifecycle management.
root.name: lifecycle_rule

root.description: Location type. Valid values: `AvailabilityZone` or `LocalZone`
root.name: location_type

root.description: Whether to create metadata configuration resource
root.name: create_metadata_configuration

root.description: The analytics source bucket ARN.
root.name: analytics_source_bucket_arn

root.description: (Optional, Default:false ) A boolean that indicates all objects should be deleted from the bucket so that the bucket can be destroyed without error. These objects are not recoverable.
root.name: force_destroy

root.description: Controls if S3 bucket should have deny non-SSL transport policy attached
root.name: attach_deny_insecure_transport_policy
